septal
  • API reference
  • AD callbacks
  • View page source

AD callbacks

JAX-CasADi callback wrappers.

Wraps arbitrary JAX functions so they can be used inside CasADi NLP problems while still benefiting from JAX’s JIT compilation and automatic differentiation.

Two AD modes are provided: - Forward mode (JaxCallbackForward / casadify_forward) — preferred when

n_constraints > n_decision (tall Jacobian).

  • Reverse mode (JaxCallbackReverse / casadify_reverse) — preferred when n_constraints <= n_decision (wide Jacobian; e.g. scalar objective).

The choice of mode is made automatically by casadify() based on the relative dimensions of the function.

class septal.casadax.callbacks.JaxCallbackForward(functn, nd, name='JaxCallbackForward', opts={})[source]

Bases: JaxCasADiEvaluator

JAX-CasADi callback using forward-mode AD (jvp).

Preferred when the number of constraints exceeds the number of decision variables (tall Jacobian).

eval(arg)[source]
get_forward(nfwd, name, inames, onames, opts)[source]
has_forward(nfwd)[source]
Return type:

bool

class septal.casadax.callbacks.JaxCallbackReverse(functn, nd, name='JaxCallbackReverse', opts={})[source]

Bases: JaxCasADiEvaluator

JAX-CasADi callback using reverse-mode AD (vjp).

Preferred when the number of constraints does not exceed the number of decision variables (wide Jacobian; e.g. scalar objective).

eval(arg)[source]
get_reverse(nadj, name, inames, onames, opts)[source]
has_reverse(nadj)[source]
Return type:

bool

class septal.casadax.callbacks.JaxCasADiEvaluator(functn, nd, name='JaxCasADiEvaluator', opts={})[source]

Bases: Callback

Base CasADi Callback that wraps a JIT-compiled JAX function.

Performs a single dummy forward pass on construction to determine the output shape so CasADi can set up its sparsity patterns.

Parameters:

  • functn (Callable) – JAX callable f(x) where x has shape (nd, 1).

  • nd (int) – Number of input dimensions.

  • name (str) – CasADi function name (must be unique within a session).

  • opts (dict) – CasADi callback options dict.

eval(arg)[source]
get_n_in()[source]
Return type:

int

get_n_out()[source]
Return type:

int

get_sparsity_in(i)[source]
Return type:

Sparsity

get_sparsity_out(i)[source]
Return type:

Sparsity

has_forward(nfwd)[source]
Return type:

bool

has_reverse(nadj)[source]
Return type:

bool

septal.casadax.callbacks.casadify(functn, nd, n_out)[source]

Auto-select forward or reverse mode based on Jacobian shape.

Parameters:

  • functn (Callable) – JAX function f(x) where x has shape (nd, 1).

  • nd (int) – Number of input dimensions.

  • n_out (int) – Number of output dimensions (rows of the Jacobian).

Return type:

JaxCasADiEvaluator

septal.casadax.callbacks.casadify_forward(functn, nd)[source]

Wrap a JAX function with forward-mode AD for use in CasADi.

Return type:

JaxCallbackForward

septal.casadax.callbacks.casadify_reverse(functn, nd)[source]

Wrap a JAX function with reverse-mode AD for use in CasADi.

Return type:

JaxCallbackReverse

Choosing AD mode

Function

AD mode

Best when

casadify_forward()

JVP (forward)

n_out > n_d (tall Jacobian)

casadify_reverse()

VJP (reverse)

n_out ≤ n_d, especially scalars

casadify()

auto-select

General use

For scalar objectives (n_out = 1), reverse mode (VJP) is always preferred.
